meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis kills more student projects than any other technical hurdle. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you inherit pre-configured authentication, database schemas, and error handling. This abstraction allows you to focus on building core features while AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code handle complex logic resolution.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal overflowing with red text, the cursor blinking in mocking silence. You’ve spent hours chasing a missing semicolon or a misconfigured environment variable, feeling the momentum of your project evaporate into frustration. Every developer has been here; the difference between the amateur and the builder is the toolset they deploy when logic fails.

This isn't about memorizing syntax; it's about architectural leverage. By adopting a professional SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start building the engine. We will use Antigravity for structural integrity and Claude Code to act as your pair programmer, transforming your debugging bottleneck into a masterclass in software engineering.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Foundation
steps.description: Initialize your project using a high-quality SaaS starter kit to ensure a clean, production-ready environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the directory structure of this starter kit and identify the core configuration files for authentication and database connectivity.

steps.title: Configure Environment Variables
steps.description: Establish your secure credentials pipeline to prevent common runtime errors caused by missing API keys.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure .env.example template that includes all necessary variables for Stripe, Supabase, and Resend integration.

steps.title: Implement Automated Error Catching
steps.description: Wrap your primary API routes in global error handling middleware to surface useful logs instead of cryptic crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unified error handling middleware for this Next.js app that logs stack traces to a local debug file.

steps.title: Isolate Logic Bugs with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a deep-dive analysis on specific functions that are causing runtime failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current function logic for user authentication; identify why the session token fails to persist on refresh.

steps.title: Deploy to Production Environment
steps.description: Push your stable build to a cloud provider to verify that your local environment matches your production configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script compatible with Vercel that includes pre-deployment linting and build checks.

pro_tips: Treat your codebase like a legal document: keep comments concise and focus on the 'why' rather than the 'how'.
Use 'Type-driven development' to let your compiler catch bugs before you even run the code.
Never debug in production; always reproduce the error in a local containerized environment first.
Master the art of the 'small commit' to isolate where a bug was introduced, making rollbacks painless.
Use AI as an educational mentor, not just a code generator; ask 'why' a fix works to internalize the pattern.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is deployed, integrate a monitoring tool like Sentry to catch bugs before your users do, then move on to adding a paid tier to your SaaS.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop debugging and start shipping. Don't let technical friction hold back your innovation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
